sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 5.41 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.5)

statement.latex: Does every non-trivial finite group, which is free in some variety, contain a non-trivial abelian normal subgroup? { A.\,Yu.\,Olshanskii} Yes, it does (mod CFSG). Otherwise, if$G$is a counterexample, the centralizer of the product$E$of all minimal normal subgroups of$G$is trivial and there is an element of$G/E$whose order is$m$, where$m$is the maximum of the orders of 2-elements of~$G$. By Theorem...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
